betterquesting:10.desc:8:
So much time. Too much time.
And yet, I still feel it flowing away from my hands.
Lives that come and go. Beauty that vanishes just as quickly as it comes to be.
And where was I? Lost in my mind, hungering for the sake of what I told myself was knowledge...
Looking at countless people as if I was in some way detached from them.
The Testificates, the Bees, my enemies. Companions through this journey.
And how many of their names do I remember? How often have I indulged in their company? Took part in their now patently fleeing lives? 
Yes, I accomplished a lot, I cannot deny it. But how present was I throughout this journey?
This doubt, this fear, is what I see now reflected before me. Was my time spent meaningfully?
Or did I just convince myself that I was doing the right thing?
This is the thing about time, isn't it? Once you are aware of it, it is already gone.
And all that can be done, well, it is to forgive, and to learn. Hoping that the next second, the next day, the next year, will be lived more meaningfully.
And to be ready to forgive, once more, for lost time.
